news_items.headline: Google just casually disrupted the open-source AI narrative…
news_items.summary:
[Fireship · 1.1M views] CodeRabbit CLI can fix your agent’s code before it ever opens a PR - https://coderabbit.link/fireship Free forever for any open source project.

Last week, Google surprised us all by shipping their latest micro model Gemma 4 under a truly open source license. But what's the catch? Let's run it...

#coding  #programming #programming

-  How Gemma 4 works
-  Gemma 4 benchmarks
- TurboQuant

news_items.headline: i didn't want to like this....
news_items.summary:
[NetworkChuck · 181K views] Try Perplexity Computer (I'm working with Perplexity on this one to give you an honest look): https://ntck.co/perplexity

Perplexity Computer is a $200/month AI system that orchestrates 19 frontier models, runs in the cloud while you sleep, and builds real apps from a single prompt. I built my own AI system — 103 skills, multiple models, AI employees — and didn't want to like it. But after building 7 real projects from my phone in Japan, I have to be honest about what happened.

news_items.headline: Oversampling Data (Explained with Audio) - Computerphile
news_items.summary:
[Computerphile · 30K views] If you run out of headroom with your chosen sample rate, how do you avoid the problems of unwanted harmonics? - Dave Domminney Fowler explains how oversampling can help. 

Computerphile is supported by Jane Street. Learn more about them (and exciting career opportunities) at: https://jane-st.co/computerphile

This video was filmed and edited by Sean Riley.

Computerphile is a sister project to Brady Haran's Numberphile. More at https://www.bradyharanblog.com

news_items.headline: Claude's Latest Update Just Made Agent Development Accessible
news_items.summary:
[Prompt Engineering · 7K views] Anthropic's new advisor strategy lets you pair Opus with Sonnet for better results at lower cost, the monitor tool kills wasteful polling loops in Claude Code, and managed agents handle the infrastructure grunt work for you. I walk through how each one works and when you should actually use them.
